bids.reasoning: It is the very beginning of the negotiation (t=0.0) and the opponent's initial offer provides a relatively low utility for me (around 0.44). Although the opponent claims to have made a generous move on issueA, their new value actually decreases my utility compared to their first offer. It's still very early in the negotiation (t=0.02), but to show some genuine flexibility and encourage a more productive exchange, I will concede by giving the opponent their repeatedly requested value on my least important issue (issueD) while holding firm on the issues that are most critical to my bottom line.

The opponent is using emotional appeals and claiming to have stretched, but the sequence of offers actually shows a decrease in utility to me with each progressing turn (dropping from 0.442 to 0.352). The opponent has consistently bid `valueC` for both `issueC` and `issueD` across multiple offers. I will concede to `valueC` for `issueC` and `issueD`, which still preserves a high utility of 0.811 for me, while remaining firm on my highest-priority items (`issueA` and `issueB`).

To counter their rigidity without making premature sacrifices, I am reverting to a previous high-utility offer (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueC) to hold firm on my high-weight issues while still offering a minor compromise on issueC and issueD.

The current time is very early (t=0.1), so I must maintain a high target utility (above 0.8). The current time fraction is very low (t=0.14), so I will maintain a high target utility (above 0.80).
